description: Demerzel has detailed beliefs about her own framework integrity but almost no beliefs about what consumer repos are actually doing with governance artifacts. The gap between self-knowledge and consumer-knowledge is the largest blind spot in the governance system. This creates a 'governance illusion' — Demerzel believes she governs, but consumer repos may not be governed in practice.
root_cause: Governance framework was built inside-out — Demerzel first, consumer integration second. This is architecturally correct (you need the framework before you can integrate it) but creates a temporal blind spot during the gap between framework completion and consumer adoption.
recommended_action: Prioritize consumer repo reconnaissance as a weekly governance activity. Create consumer compliance dashboard. Establish minimum viable governance for each consumer: submodule current, state/ dir exists, CLAUDE.md governance section present, at least one belief state populated.
constitutional_articles: Article 2 (Transparency) — the gap between governance claim and governance reality is opaque
Article 8 (Observability) — consumer repo governance health is unobservable
